recipes.title: Machboos
recipes.description: Emirati national rice dish of basmati spiced with loomi, cardamom, and saffron, layered with chicken or lamb.
steps: Brown chicken pieces in oil with chopped onions until golden.
Stir in tomato paste, bezar, turmeric, garlic, and grated ginger; cook 2 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes, dried limes pierced with a knife, salt, and 1.2 liters water.
Simmer covered for 35 minutes until chicken is tender; remove chicken and broil 8 minutes to crisp.
Soak rice 20 minutes, then add to the broth and simmer covered on low heat for 18 minutes.
Bloom saffron in 2 tablespoons hot water and drizzle over the rice in the last 5 minutes.
Mound rice on a platter, top with chicken and chopped cilantro.

recipes.title: Harees
recipes.description: Emirati Ramadan staple of pounded wheat berries slow-cooked with chicken or lamb into a creamy savory porridge.
steps: Soak wheat berries overnight in plenty of water.
Simmer drained wheat with chicken, chopped onion, cardamom, salt, and 2.5 liters water for 3 hours.
Remove the bones and shred the chicken back into the pot.
Beat the mixture with a wooden spoon for 20 minutes until very smooth and stringy.
Stir in cinnamon and adjust salt; the texture should be like a thick porridge.
Spoon into bowls and pour melted ghee generously over the top.
Serve hot for iftar.

recipes.title: Luqaimat
recipes.description: Emirati golden yeast-risen dumplings deep fried crisp and drizzled with date syrup; a Ramadan favorite.
steps: Bloom saffron in 2 tablespoons of hot water.
Mix flour, salt, cardamom, yeast, sugar, yogurt, saffron water, and warm water into a thick batter.
Cover and rest 1.5 hours until bubbly and doubled.
Heat oil to 170C in a deep pot.
Pinch small balls of batter from a wet hand and drop into the oil.
Fry for 4 minutes, turning often, until deep golden and hollow inside.
Drain on paper and immediately drizzle with date syrup and shower with sesame seeds.
Serve hot.

recipes.title: Balaleet
recipes.description: Emirati sweet-savory breakfast of saffron-cardamom vermicelli noodles topped with a thin omelette and rosewater.
steps: Bloom saffron in 3 tablespoons hot water.
Toast the vermicelli in 30 g ghee in a wide skillet until deep golden, breaking into shorter pieces.
Add 600 ml water, sugar, cardamom, and saffron water; bring to a boil and cover.
Simmer on lowest heat for 8 minutes until water is absorbed and noodles are tender.
Stir in rosewater and the remaining ghee.
Beat eggs with a pinch of salt; cook in a buttered skillet as a thin flat omelette and slice into ribbons.
Mound the saffron noodles on a plate and top with the egg ribbons.
Serve warm with extra date syrup if desired.

recipes.title: Khabeesa
recipes.description: Emirati pudding of toasted flour cooked with sugar, saffron, cardamom, and rosewater into a fragrant fudgy dessert.
steps: Toast the flour in a dry pan over medium heat for 12 minutes, stirring constantly, until deep golden and nutty.
Heat sugar with water, saffron, and cardamom until dissolved into a fragrant syrup.
Melt ghee in the pan with the toasted flour over low heat and stir to combine.
Slowly stream in the hot syrup while stirring vigorously to prevent lumps.
Cook for 8 minutes, stirring, until the mixture is glossy, thick, and pulling away from the pan.
Stir in rosewater off the heat.
Spread on a serving plate and decorate with chopped almonds and pistachios.
Serve warm with Arabic coffee.

recipes.title: Madrouba
recipes.description: Emirati savory porridge of rice and chicken slow-cooked with milk and butter until very smooth and flavorful.
steps: Brown chicken with chopped onion in oil; stir in bezar, turmeric, and dried limes.
Add chopped tomatoes, salt, and 1.5 liters water; simmer 30 minutes.
Remove chicken and shred; discard bones and dried limes.
Add washed rice to the broth and simmer 25 minutes until very soft.
Stir in the milk and shredded chicken; continue cooking, beating with a spoon, until smooth and porridge-like.
Stir in butter and adjust salt.
Serve hot with extra ghee on top.

recipes.title: Thareed
recipes.description: Emirati Ramadan stew of lamb, vegetables, and dried lime ladled over crisp regag bread to soak up the broth.
steps: Brown cubed lamb with chopped onions in oil.
Stir in tomato paste, bezar, and pierced dried limes.
Add chopped tomatoes, salt, and 1.5 liters water; simmer 1 hour.
Add cubed carrots, potatoes, courgettes, and pumpkin; simmer 25 minutes more.
Tear regag bread into rough pieces and pile in a deep serving bowl.
Ladle the stew with vegetables over the bread and let soak 5 minutes.
Serve hot for iftar.

recipes.title: Khanfaroosh
recipes.description: Emirati cardamom-saffron yeasted donuts with a slightly chewy crumb, fried golden and dusted with sesame.
steps: Bloom saffron in 2 tablespoons hot water.
Warm milk with sugar to dissolve, add yeast, and let foam for 10 minutes.
Whisk eggs, cardamom, salt, saffron water, rosewater, and the milk mixture.
Stir in both flours to make a thick smooth batter.
Cover and rest in a warm place for 1 hour until bubbly and risen.
Heat oil to 170C.
Drop heaped tablespoons of batter into the oil and fry for 4 minutes until deep golden and cooked through.
Drain on paper and toss with sesame seeds while warm.

recipes.title: Chebab
recipes.description: Emirati saffron and cardamom pancakes with date syrup and fresh cheese, a beloved breakfast across the UAE.
steps: Bloom saffron in 2 tablespoons hot water.
Warm milk slightly and dissolve sugar and yeast; let foam for 10 minutes.
Whisk eggs, cardamom, rosewater, salt, saffron water, and the milk mixture.
Stir in flour to a smooth pourable batter.
Rest covered for 45 minutes.
Heat a non-stick pan with a thin smear of ghee.
Pour 60 ml batter and cook for 90 seconds until bubbles set, then flip for 30 seconds.
Serve warm with date syrup and cream cheese.
